21.reason: [The judge function logic is incorrect: it returns 1 even when x and y are of different types (e.g., one digit and one letter), violating the requirement that both sides must be same type]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.25 , reason:  Missing check that first[i+1] > first[i-1]; also judge function is incorrect as noted; additionally, no bounds check for i-1 and i+1 (potential out-of-range access).
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: No check that i-1>=0 and i+1<len to avoid out-of-bounds access. Also judge lacks condition right > left; without that, equal or smaller cases incorrectly enter block and omit '-'.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.25 , reason:  Missing condition: fails to check if the character after '-' is greater than the one before it, as explicitly required by the problem statement.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Logical error: missing condition first[i+1] > first[i-1]. Expansion must only occur when the character after '-' is strictly greater than the one before it.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [The judge function logic is incorrect: it returns 1 even when x and y are of different types (e.g., one digit and one letter), violating the requirement that both sides must be same type]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [Logic error: judge() incorrectly returns 1 even when x and y are of different types (e.g., one digit and one letter). Also, accesses first[i-1] when i=0 and first[i+1] when i=len-1, causing out-of-bounds access.]

23.reason: [No check that first[i+1] > first[i-1]; if not, loop condition fails or causes negative/invalid behavior. Also, if i is 0 or i+1 is beyond string, accesses invalid memory]

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.2 , reason:  Error: If first[i+1] <= first[i-1], loop does nothing but '-' is not printed; should only expand when right > left.
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-1: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [No check that first[i+1] > first[i-1]; if not, loop condition fails or causes negative/invalid behavior. Also, if i is 0 or i+1 is beyond string, accesses invalid memory]
reasons.buggy-qwen3-max-2: 0.3333333333333333 , reason:  [If first[i+1] <= first[i-1], loop condition fails to prevent invalid expansion; also relies on prior flawed type check.]
